slots.copy: See the HubSpot customer platform work for your team.

slots.name: support

slots.name: see-points
copy: Breeze AI agents working a real pipeline, live
Marketing, sales, and service on one CRM
30 minutes, tailored to the tools you already run

copy.note: We'll email you a scheduling link within one business day — pick a time that works, no obligation.

rationale: One hero only. The brief is a lead-gen opener, so I chose the hero-form-split genre archetype: a persuasion column (kicker, display heading with the platform claim, a lede that states exactly what you'll see and how long, a scannable value list, and a 299,000+ proof stat) beside the form as the counterweight in cols 7-12. The form carries only first name, work email, company size and one clear 'Book my demo' submit, with a note that says precisely what happens next (scheduling link within a business day) — never gating behind vague promises. Split archetype keeps modules on the shared 12-col grid; alignment is left to the brand's header grammar. Per brand surface rhythm this opening bookend is the single inverse (deep-teal) band and the only section carrying the accent, applied to the display title. No text logos, no dark mid-run bands, sentence-case heading at weight 500 — every neverDo holds by construction. Novel because the seed library's hero pattern is a photo overlay, not a capture form; recomposed within the drawable split archetype.
